118	class SamplingParams(
119	        msgspec.Struct,
120	        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
121	        # required for @cached_property.
122	        dict=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
123	    """Sampling parameters for text generation.
124	
125	    Overall, we follow the sampling parameters from the OpenAI text completion
126	    API (https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create).
127	    In addition, we support beam search, which is not supported by OpenAI.
128	
129	    Args:
130	        n: Number of output sequences to return for the given prompt.
131	        best_of: Number of output sequences that are generated from the prompt.
132	            From these `best_of` sequences, the top `n` sequences are returned.
133	            `best_of` must be greater than or equal to `n`. By default,
134	            `best_of` is set to `n`. Warning, this is only supported in V0.
135	        presence_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether they
136	            appear in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the model
137	            to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to repeat
138	            tokens.
139	        frequency_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on their
140	            frequency in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the
141	            model to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to
142	            repeat tokens.
143	        repetition_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether
144	            they appear in the prompt and the generated text so far. Values > 1
145	            encourage the model to use new tokens, while values < 1 encourage
146	            the model to repeat tokens.
147	        temperature: Float that controls the randomness of the sampling. Lower
148	            values make the model more deterministic, while higher values make
149	            the model more random. Zero means greedy sampling.
150	        top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
151	            to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
152	        top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
153	            to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
154	        min_p: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be
155	            considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token.
156	            Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.
157	        seed: Random seed to use for the generation.
158	        stop: list of strings that stop the generation when they are generated.
159	            The returned output will not contain the stop strings.
160	        stop_token_ids: list of tokens that stop the generation when they are
161	            generated. The returned output will contain the stop tokens unless
162	            the stop tokens are special tokens.
163	        bad_words: list of words that are not allowed to be generated.
164	            More precisely, only the last token of a corresponding
165	            token sequence is not allowed when the next generated token
166	            can complete the sequence.
167	        include_stop_str_in_output: Whether to include the stop strings in
168	            output text. Defaults to False.
169	        ignore_eos: Whether to ignore the EOS token and continue generating
170	            tokens after the EOS token is generated.
171	        max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate per output sequence.
172	        min_tokens: Minimum number of tokens to generate per output sequence
173	            before EOS or stop_token_ids can be generated
174	        logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per output token.
175	            When set to None, no probability is returned. If set to a non-None
176	            value, the result includes the log probabilities of the specified
177	            number of most likely tokens, as well as the chosen tokens.
178	            Note that the implementation follows the OpenAI API: The API will
179	            always return the log probability of the sampled token, so there
180	            may be up to `logprobs+1` elements in the response.
181	        prompt_logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per prompt token.
182	        detokenize: Whether to detokenize the output. Defaults to True.
183	        skip_special_tokens: Whether to skip special tokens in the output.
184	        spaces_between_special_tokens: Whether to add spaces between special
185	            tokens in the output.  Defaults to True.
186	        logits_processors: list of functions that modify logits based on
187	            previously generated tokens, and optionally prompt tokens as
188	            a first argument.
189	        truncate_prompt_tokens: If set to -1, will use the truncation size
190	            supported by the model. If set to an integer k, will use only
191	            the last k tokens from the prompt (i.e., left truncation).
192	            Defaults to None (i.e., no truncation).
193	        guided_decoding: If provided, the engine will construct a guided
194	            decoding logits processor from these parameters. Defaults to None.
195	        logit_bias: If provided, the engine will construct a logits processor
196	            that applies these logit biases. Defaults to None.
197	        allowed_token_ids: If provided, the engine will construct a logits
198	            processor which only retains scores for the given token ids.
199	            Defaults to None.
200	        extra_args: Arbitrary additional args, that can be used by custom
201	            sampling implementations, plugins, etc. Not used by any in-tree
202	            sampling implementations.
203	    """
204	
205	    n: int = 1
206	    best_of: Optional[int] = None
207	    _real_n: Optional[int] = None
208	    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
209	    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
210	    repetition_penalty: float = 1.0
211	    temperature: float = 1.0
212	    top_p: float = 1.0
213	    top_k: int = 0
214	    min_p: float = 0.0
215	    seed: Optional[int] = None
216	    stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None
217	    stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
218	    ignore_eos: bool = False
219	    max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16
220	    min_tokens: int = 0
221	    logprobs: Optional[int] = None
222	    prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None
223	    # NOTE: This parameter is only exposed at the engine level for now.
224	    # It is not exposed in the OpenAI API server, as the OpenAI API does
225	    # not support returning only a list of token IDs.
226	    detokenize: bool = True
227	    skip_special_tokens: bool = True
228	    spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True
229	    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type. We use Any here because
230	    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type is not supported by msgspec.
231	    logits_processors: Optional[Any] = None
232	    include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False
233	    truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int, msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None
234	    output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE
235	
236	    # The below fields are not supposed to be used as an input.
237	    # They are set in post_init.
238	    output_text_buffer_length: int = 0
239	    _all_stop_token_ids: set[int] = msgspec.field(default_factory=set)
240	
241	    # Fields used to construct logits processors
242	    guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None
243	    logit_bias: Optional[dict[int, float]] = None
244	    allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
245	    extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None
246	
247	    # Fields used for bad words
248	    bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None
249	    _bad_words_token_ids: Optional[list[list[int]]] = None
250	
251	    @staticmethod
252	    def from_optional(
253	        n: Optional[int] = 1,
254	        best_of: Optional[int] = None,
255	        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
256	        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
257	        repetition_penalty: Optional[float] = 1.0,
258	        temperature: Optional[float] = 1.0,
259	        top_p: Optional[float] = 1.0,
260	        top_k: int = 0,
261	        min_p: float = 0.0,
262	        seed: Optional[int] = None,
263	        stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None,
264	        stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None,
265	        bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None,
266	        include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False,
267	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
268	        max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16,
269	        min_tokens: int = 0,
270	        logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
271	        prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
272	        detokenize: bool = True,
273	        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
274	        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
275	        logits_processors: Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] = None,
276	        truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int,
277	                                                   msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None,
278	        output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE,
279	        guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None,
280	        logit_bias: Optional[Union[dict[int, float], dict[str, float]]] = None,
281	        allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None,
282	        extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
283	    ) -> "SamplingParams":
284	        if logit_bias is not None:
285	            # Convert token_id to integer
286	            # Clamp the bias between -100 and 100 per OpenAI API spec
287	            logit_bias = {
288	                int(token): min(100.0, max(-100.0, bias))
289	                for token, bias in logit_bias.items()
290	            }
291	
292	        return SamplingParams(
293	            n=1 if n is None else n,
294	            best_of=best_of,
295	            presence_penalty=0.0
296	            if presence_penalty is None else presence_penalty,
297	            frequency_penalty=0.0
298	            if frequency_penalty is None else frequency_penalty,
299	            repetition_penalty=1.0
300	            if repetition_penalty is None else repetition_penalty,
301	            temperature=1.0 if temperature is None else temperature,
302	            top_p=1.0 if top_p is None else top_p,
303	            top_k=top_k,
304	            min_p=min_p,
305	            seed=seed,
306	            stop=stop,
307	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
308	            bad_words=bad_words,
309	            include_stop_str_in_output=include_stop_str_in_output,
310	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
311	            max_tokens=max_tokens,
312	            min_tokens=min_tokens,
313	            logprobs=logprobs,
314	            prompt_logprobs=prompt_logprobs,
315	            detokenize=detokenize,
316	            skip_special_tokens=skip_special_tokens,
317	            spaces_between_special_tokens=spaces_between_special_tokens,
318	            logits_processors=logits_processors,
319	            truncate_prompt_tokens=truncate_prompt_tokens,
320	            output_kind=output_kind,
321	            guided_decoding=guided_decoding,
322	            logit_bias=logit_bias,
323	            allowed_token_ids=allowed_token_ids,
324	            extra_args=extra_args,
325	        )
326	
327	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
328	        # how we deal with `best_of``:
329	        # if `best_of`` is not set, we default to `n`;
330	        # if `best_of`` is set, we set `n`` to `best_of`,
331	        # and set `_real_n`` to the original `n`.
332	        # when we return the result, we will check
333	        # if we need to return `n` or `_real_n` results
334	        if self.best_of:
335	            if self.best_of < self.n:
336	                raise ValueError(
337	                    f"best_of must be greater than or equal to n, "
338	                    f"got n={self.n} and best_of={self.best_of}.")
339	            if not self._real_n:
340	                self._real_n = self.n
341	                self.n = self.best_of
342	
343	        if 0 < self.temperature < _MAX_TEMP:
344	            logger.warning(
345	                "temperature %s is less than %s, which may cause numerical "
346	                "errors nan or inf in tensors. We have maxed it out to %s.",
347	                self.temperature, _MAX_TEMP, _MAX_TEMP)
348	            self.temperature = max(self.temperature, _MAX_TEMP)
349	
350	        if self.seed == -1:
351	            self.seed = None
352	
353	        if self.stop is None:
354	            self.stop = []
355	        elif isinstance(self.stop, str):
356	            self.stop = [self.stop]
357	
358	        if self.stop_token_ids is None:
359	            self.stop_token_ids = []
360	
361	        if self.bad_words is None:
362	            self.bad_words = []
363	
364	        if self.logprobs is True:
365	            self.logprobs = 1
366	
367	        if self.prompt_logprobs is True:
368	            self.prompt_logprobs = 1
369	
370	        # Number of characters to hold back for stop string evaluation
371	        # until sequence is finished.
372	        if self.stop and not self.include_stop_str_in_output:
373	            self.output_text_buffer_length = max(len(s) for s in self.stop) - 1
374	
375	        self._verify_args()
376	
377	        if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
378	            # Zero temperature means greedy sampling.
379	            self.top_p = 1.0
380	            self.top_k = 0
381	            self.min_p = 0.0
382	            self._verify_greedy_sampling()
383	
384	        # eos_token_id is added to this by the engine
385	        self._all_stop_token_ids.update(self.stop_token_ids)
386	
387	    def _verify_args(self) -> None:
388	        if not isinstance(self.n, int):
389	            raise ValueError(f"n must be an int, but is of "
390	                             f"type {type(self.n)}")
391	        if self.n < 1:
392	            raise ValueError(f"n must be at least 1, got {self.n}.")
393	        if self.best_of is not None:
394	            if not isinstance(self.best_of, int):
395	                raise ValueError(
396	                    f"best_of must be an integer, got {type(self.best_of)}")
397	            if self.best_of < 1:
398	                raise ValueError(
399	                    f"best_of must be at least 1, got {self.best_of}")
400	            if self.best_of < self.n:
401	                raise ValueError(
402	                    f"best_of must be greater than or equal to n, "
403	                    f"got n={self.n} and best_of={self.best_of}.")
404	        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
405	            raise ValueError("presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
406	                             f"{self.presence_penalty}.")
407	        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
408	            raise ValueError("frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
409	                             f"{self.frequency_penalty}.")
410	        if self.repetition_penalty <= 0.0:
411	            raise ValueError(
412	                "repetition_penalty must be greater than zero, got "
413	                f"{self.repetition_penalty}.")
414	        if self.temperature < 0.0:
415	            raise ValueError(
416	                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}.")
417	        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
418	            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
419	        # quietly accept -1 as disabled, but prefer 0
420	        if self.top_k < -1:
421	            raise ValueError(f"top_k must be 0 (disable), or at least 1, "
422	                             f"got {self.top_k}.")
423	        if not isinstance(self.top_k, int):
424	            raise TypeError(
425	                f"top_k must be an integer, got {type(self.top_k).__name__}")
426	        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
427	            raise ValueError("min_p must be in [0, 1], got "
428	                             f"{self.min_p}.")
429	        if self.max_tokens is not None and self.max_tokens < 1:
430	            raise ValueError(
431	                f"max_tokens must be at least 1, got {self.max_tokens}.")
432	        if self.min_tokens < 0:
433	            raise ValueError(f"min_tokens must be greater than or equal to 0, "
434	                             f"got {self.min_tokens}.")
435	        if self.max_tokens is not None and self.min_tokens > self.max_tokens:
436	            raise ValueError(
437	                f"min_tokens must be less than or equal to "
438	                f"max_tokens={self.max_tokens}, got {self.min_tokens}.")
439	        if self.logprobs is not None and self.logprobs < 0:
440	            raise ValueError(
441	                f"logprobs must be non-negative, got {self.logprobs}.")
442	        if self.prompt_logprobs is not None and self.prompt_logprobs < 0:
443	            raise ValueError(f"prompt_logprobs must be non-negative, got "
444	                             f"{self.prompt_logprobs}.")
445	        if (self.truncate_prompt_tokens is not None
446	                and self.truncate_prompt_tokens < 1):
447	            raise ValueError(f"truncate_prompt_tokens must be >= 1, "
448	                             f"got {self.truncate_prompt_tokens}")
449	        assert isinstance(self.stop_token_ids, list)
450	        if not all(isinstance(st_id, int) for st_id in self.stop_token_ids):
451	            raise ValueError(f"stop_token_ids must contain only integers, "
452	                             f"got {self.stop_token_ids}.")
453	        assert isinstance(self.stop, list)
454	        if any(not stop_str for stop_str in self.stop):
455	            raise ValueError("stop cannot contain an empty string.")
456	        if self.stop and not self.detokenize:
457	            raise ValueError(
458	                "stop strings are only supported when detokenize is True. "
459	                "Set detokenize=True to use stop.")
460	        if self.best_of != self._real_n and self.output_kind == (
461	                RequestOutputKind.DELTA):
462	            raise ValueError("best_of must equal n to use output_kind=DELTA")
463	
464	    def _verify_greedy_sampling(self) -> None:
465	        if self.n > 1:
466	            raise ValueError("n must be 1 when using greedy sampling, "
467	                             f"got {self.n}.")
468	
469	    def update_from_generation_config(
470	            self,
471	            generation_config: dict[str, Any],
472	            model_eos_token_id: Optional[int] = None) -> None:
473	        """Update if there are non-default values from generation_config"""
474	
475	        if model_eos_token_id is not None:
476	            # Add the eos token id into the sampling_params to support
477	            # min_tokens processing.
478	            self._all_stop_token_ids.add(model_eos_token_id)
479	
480	        # Update eos_token_id for generation
481	        if (eos_ids := generation_config.get("eos_token_id")) is not None:
482	            # it can be either int or list of int
483	            eos_ids = {eos_ids} if isinstance(eos_ids, int) else set(eos_ids)
484	            if model_eos_token_id is not None:
485	                # We don't need to include the primary eos_token_id in
486	                # stop_token_ids since it's handled separately for stopping
487	                # purposes.
488	                eos_ids.discard(model_eos_token_id)
489	            if eos_ids:
490	                self._all_stop_token_ids.update(eos_ids)
491	                if not self.ignore_eos:
492	                    eos_ids.update(self.stop_token_ids)
493	                    self.stop_token_ids = list(eos_ids)
494	
495	    def update_from_tokenizer(self, tokenizer: AnyTokenizer) -> None:
496	        if not self.bad_words:
497	            return
498	        self._bad_words_token_ids = []
499	        for bad_word in self.bad_words:
500	            # To prohibit words both at the beginning
501	            # and in the middle of text
502	            # (related to add_prefix_space tokenizer parameter)
503	            for add_prefix_space in [False, True]:
504	                prefix = " " if add_prefix_space else ""
505	                prompt = prefix + bad_word.lstrip()
506	                prompt_token_ids = tokenizer.encode(text=prompt,
507	                                                    add_special_tokens=False)
508	
509	                # If no space at the beginning
510	                # or if prefix space produces a new word token
511	                if (not add_prefix_space) or (
512	                        add_prefix_space and prompt_token_ids[0]
513	                        != self._bad_words_token_ids[-1][0]
514	                        and len(prompt_token_ids) == len(
515	                            self._bad_words_token_ids[-1])):
516	                    self._bad_words_token_ids.append(prompt_token_ids)
517	
518	        invalid_token_ids = [
519	            token_id for bad_words_token_ids in self._bad_words_token_ids
520	            for token_id in bad_words_token_ids
521	            if token_id < 0 or token_id > tokenizer.max_token_id
522	        ]
523	        if len(invalid_token_ids) > 0:
524	            raise ValueError(
525	                f"The model vocabulary size is {tokenizer.max_token_id+1},"
526	                f" but the following tokens"
527	                f" were specified as bad: {invalid_token_ids}."
528	                f" All token id values should be integers satisfying:"
529	                f" 0 <= token_id <= {tokenizer.max_token_id}.")
530	
531	    @cached_property
532	    def sampling_type(self) -> SamplingType:
533	        if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
534	            return SamplingType.GREEDY
535	        if self.seed is not None:
536	            return SamplingType.RANDOM_SEED
537	        return SamplingType.RANDOM
538	
539	    @property
540	    def all_stop_token_ids(self) -> set[int]:
541	        return self._all_stop_token_ids
542	
543	    @property
544	    def bad_words_token_ids(self) -> Optional[list[list[int]]]:
545	        # For internal use only. Backward compatibility not guaranteed
546	        return self._bad_words_token_ids
547	
548	    def clone(self) -> "SamplingParams":
549	        """Deep copy, but maybe not the LogitsProcessor objects.
550	
551	        LogitsProcessor objects may contain an arbitrary, nontrivial amount of
552	        data that is expensive to copy. However, if not copied, the processor
553	        needs to support parallel decoding for multiple sequences
554	        See https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/3087
555	        """
556	
557	        logit_processor_refs = None if self.logits_processors is None else {
558	            id(lp): lp.clone() if hasattr(lp, 'clone') else lp
559	            for lp in self.logits_processors
560	        }
561	        return copy.deepcopy(self, memo=logit_processor_refs)
562	
563	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
564	        return (
565	            f"SamplingParams(n={self.n}, "
566	            f"presence_penalty={self.presence_penalty}, "
567	            f"frequency_penalty={self.frequency_penalty}, "
568	            f"repetition_penalty={self.repetition_penalty}, "
569	            f"temperature={self.temperature}, "
570	            f"top_p={self.top_p}, "
571	            f"top_k={self.top_k}, "
572	            f"min_p={self.min_p}, "
573	            f"seed={self.seed}, "
574	            f"stop={self.stop}, "
575	            f"stop_token_ids={self.stop_token_ids}, "
576	            f"bad_words={self.bad_words}, "
577	            f"include_stop_str_in_output={self.include_stop_str_in_output}, "
578	            f"ignore_eos={self.ignore_eos}, "
579	            f"max_tokens={self.max_tokens}, "
580	            f"min_tokens={self.min_tokens}, "
581	            f"logprobs={self.logprobs}, "
582	            f"prompt_logprobs={self.prompt_logprobs}, "
583	            f"skip_special_tokens={self.skip_special_tokens}, "
584	            "spaces_between_special_tokens="
585	            f"{self.spaces_between_special_tokens}, "
586	            f"truncate_prompt_tokens={self.truncate_prompt_tokens}, "
587	            f"guided_decoding={self.guided_decoding}, "
588	            f"extra_args={self.extra_args})")
589

690	class SequenceGroupState(msgspec.Struct,
691	                         omit_defaults=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
692	    """Mutable state tied to a specific sequence group"""
693	
694	    # for multi-step decoding
695	    num_steps: int = 1
696	    current_step: int = 0
697	
698	    @property
699	    def remaining_steps(self) -> int:
700	        return self.num_steps - self.current_step

702	
703	class SequenceGroup:
704	    """A group of sequences that are generated from the same prompt.
705	
706	    Args:
707	        request_id: The ID of the request.
708	        seqs: The list of sequences.
709	        sampling_params: The sampling parameters used to generate the outputs.
710	        arrival_time: The arrival time of the request.
711	        lora_request: LoRA request.
712	        pooling_params: The parameters used to generate the pooler
713	            for a pooling model.
714	        pooled_data: The extracted hidden states from a pooling model.
715	        encoder_seq: Optional, the single encoder sequence. Should be None
716	                     unless you are working with an encoder/decoder model.
717	        trace_headers: OpenTelemetry trace headers.
718	        prompt_adapter_request: Prompt Adapter request.
719	        priority: User-defined priority of the request.
720	        draft_size: The number of speculative tokens plus one from the target
721	                    model; equal to max number of tokens a step can generate
722	                    for single-draft speculative decoding but larger than
723	                    that for multi-draft SD (currently not supported).
724	    """
725	
726	    def __init__(self,
727	                 request_id: str,
728	                 seqs: list[Sequence],
729	                 arrival_time: float,
730	                 sampling_params: Optional[SamplingParams] = None,
731	                 lora_request: Optional[LoRARequest] = None,
732	                 pooling_params: Optional[PoolingParams] = None,
733	                 pooled_data: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
734	                 encoder_seq: Optional[Sequence] = None,
735	                 trace_headers: Optional[Mapping[str, str]] = None,
736	                 prompt_adapter_request: Optional[PromptAdapterRequest] = None,
737	                 priority: int = 0,
738	                 draft_size: int = 1) -> None:
739	        self.request_id = request_id
740	        self.seqs = seqs
741	        self.first_seq = seqs[0]
742	        self.arrival_time = arrival_time
743	        self.is_single_seq = len(seqs) == 1
744	        self.seqs_dict = {seq.seq_id: seq for seq in seqs}
745	
746	        self.sampling_params = sampling_params
747	        self.metrics = RequestMetrics(arrival_time=arrival_time,
748	                                      last_token_time=arrival_time,
749	                                      first_scheduled_time=None,
750	                                      first_token_time=None,
751	                                      time_in_queue=None,
752	                                      spec_token_acceptance_counts=[0] *
753	                                      draft_size)
754	        self.last_token_latency = 0.0
755	        self.lora_request = lora_request
756	        self.prompt_logprobs: Optional[PromptLogprobs] = None
757	        self.state = SequenceGroupState()
758	        self.pooling_params = pooling_params
759	        self.pooled_data = pooled_data
760	        self.prompt_adapter_request = prompt_adapter_request
761	        self.encoder_seq = encoder_seq
762	        self.trace_headers = trace_headers
763	        self.priority = priority
764	
765	        self.cached_request_output = None
766	
767	    @property
768	    def prompt(self) -> Optional[str]:
769	        return self.first_seq.prompt
770	
771	    @property
772	    def prompt_token_ids(self) -> list[int]:
773	        return self.first_seq.prompt_token_ids
774	
775	    @property
776	    def encoder_prompt(self) -> Optional[str]:
777	        # There are either 0 or 1 encoder sequences
778	        # If one is present, its prompt is distinct
779	        # from the decoder's.
780	        return (self.encoder_seq.prompt
781	                if self.encoder_seq is not None else None)
782	
783	    @property
784	    def encoder_prompt_token_ids(self) -> Optional[list[int]]:
785	        # There are either 0 or 1 encoder sequences
786	        # If one is present, its prompt token ids are
787	        # distinct from the decoder's.
788	        return (self.encoder_seq.prompt_token_ids
789	                if self.encoder_seq is not None else None)
790	
791	    @property
792	    def token_type_ids(self) -> Optional[list[int]]:
793	        return self.first_seq.token_type_ids
794	
795	    @property
796	    def multi_modal_data(self) -> MultiModalKwargs:
797	        if self.first_seq.multi_modal_data:
798	            return self.first_seq.multi_modal_data
799	        elif self.encoder_seq is not None:
800	            return self.encoder_seq.multi_modal_data
801	        return MultiModalKwargs({})
802	
803	    @property
804	    def multi_modal_placeholders(self) -> MultiModalPlaceholderDict:
805	        if self.first_seq.multi_modal_data:
806	            return self.first_seq.multi_modal_placeholders
807	        elif self.encoder_seq is not None:
808	            return self.encoder_seq.multi_modal_placeholders
809	        return {}
810	
811	    @property
812	    def lora_int_id(self) -> int:
813	        return self.lora_request.lora_int_id if self.lora_request else 0
814	
815	    @property
816	    def prompt_adapter_id(self) -> int:
817	        return self.prompt_adapter_request.prompt_adapter_id \
818	                        if self.prompt_adapter_request else 0
819	
820	    @property
821	    def prompt_adapter_num_virtual_tokens(self) -> int:
822	        return self.prompt_adapter_request.prompt_adapter_num_virtual_tokens\
823	                         if self.prompt_adapter_request else 0
824	
825	    def init_multi_step(self, num_steps: int) -> None:
826	        self.state.num_steps = num_steps
827	        self.state.current_step = 0
828	
829	    def init_multi_step_from_lookahead_slots(self, num_lookahead_slots: int,
830	                                             num_scheduler_steps: int,
831	                                             is_multi_step: bool,
832	                                             enable_chunking: bool) -> None:
833	
834	        if not is_multi_step:
835	            self.init_multi_step(num_steps=num_scheduler_steps)
836	            return
837	
838	        # Multi-Step case
839	        is_prefill = self.is_prefill()
840	
841	        # The asserts below reflect the expectations of the current system.
842	        if is_prefill and enable_chunking:
843	            assert num_lookahead_slots == num_scheduler_steps
844	            self.init_multi_step(num_steps=num_lookahead_slots)
845	        else:
846	            is_decode: bool = not is_prefill
847	            # If it is a prefill, num_lookahead_slots must be 0
848	            assert num_lookahead_slots == 0 or is_decode
849	            # If it is a decode, num_lookahead_slots + 1 must match
850	            # the scheduler steps.
851	            assert num_lookahead_slots + 1 == num_scheduler_steps or is_prefill
852	            self.init_multi_step(num_steps=num_lookahead_slots + 1)
853	
854	    def set_last_token_time(self, now: float) -> None:
855	        """Sets the last token time for Request level timings."""
856	        # If still in prefill phase, assertion fails.
857	        assert not self.is_prefill(), (
858	            "seq_group.set_last_token_time() should not be called "
859	            "if the seq_group is in prefill phase.")
860	        self.last_token_latency = now - self.metrics.last_token_time
861	        self.metrics.last_token_time = now
862	
863	    def get_last_token_latency(self) -> float:
864	        """Returns the latency of the last token."""
865	        assert not self.is_prefill(), (
866	            "seq_group.get_last_token_latency() should not be called "
867	            "if the seq_group is in prefill phase.")
868	        return self.last_token_latency
869	
870	    def maybe_set_first_token_time(self, time: float) -> None:
871	        """Sets the first token time for Request level timings."""
872	        # Note: in a case where a sequence_group is swapped and
873	        #   recomputed, the time between iterations is counted
874	        #   in TPOT, rather than recalculating TTFT (since from the )
875	        #   POV of the user, there is simply a long generation delay.
876	        if (self.metrics.first_token_time is None
877	                and self.first_seq.get_output_len() == 1):
878	            self.metrics.first_token_time = time
879	
880	    def maybe_set_first_scheduled_time(self, time: float) -> None:
881	        """Sets the first scheduled time and time in queue for Request
882	        level timings."""
883	        if self.metrics.first_scheduled_time is None:
884	            self.metrics.first_scheduled_time = time
885	            self.metrics.time_in_queue = time - self.metrics.arrival_time
886	
887	    def set_finished_time(self, time: Optional[float]) -> None:
888	        """Sets the finished time for Request level timings."""
889	        self.metrics.finished_time = time
890	
891	    def get_max_num_running_seqs(self) -> int:
892	        """The maximum number of sequences running in parallel in the remaining
893	        lifetime of the request."""
894	        if self.is_single_seq:
895	            return 0 if self.first_seq.is_finished() else 1
896	        return self.num_seqs() - self.num_finished_seqs()
897	
898	    def get_seqs(
899	        self,
900	        status: Optional[SequenceStatus] = None,
901	    ) -> list[Sequence]:
902	        if status is None:
903	            return self.seqs
904	
905	        if self.is_single_seq:
906	            return self.seqs if self.first_seq.status == status else []
907	
908	        return [seq for seq in self.seqs if seq.status == status]
909	
910	    def is_encoder_decoder(self) -> bool:
911	        return self.encoder_seq is not None
912	
913	    def get_encoder_seq(self) -> Optional[Sequence]:
914	        return self.encoder_seq
915	
916	    def get_finished_seqs(self) -> list[Sequence]:
917	        if self.is_single_seq:
918	            return self.seqs if self.first_seq.is_finished() else []
919	
920	        return [seq for seq in self.seqs if seq.is_finished()]
921	
922	    def update_num_computed_tokens(self, num_new_computed_tokens: int):
923	        """Update number of tokens computed so far."""
924	        for seq in self.seqs:
925	            if not seq.is_finished():
926	                seq.data.update_num_computed_tokens(num_new_computed_tokens)
927	
928	    def get_num_uncomputed_tokens(self) -> int:
929	        num_uncomputed_tokens = 0
930	        for seq in self.seqs:
931	            if not seq.is_finished():
932	                num_uncomputed_tokens += seq.data.get_num_uncomputed_tokens()
933	        return num_uncomputed_tokens
934	
935	    def num_seqs(self, status: Optional[SequenceStatus] = None) -> int:
936	        # Optimization. We don't need to call get_seqs if we don't need to
937	        # filter by states.
938	        if status is None:
939	            return len(self.seqs)
940	
941	        if self.is_single_seq:
942	            return 1 if self.seqs[0].status == status else 0
943	
944	        return len(self.get_seqs(status))
945	
946	    def num_finished_seqs(self) -> int:
947	        if self.is_single_seq:
948	            return 1 if self.seqs[0].is_finished() else 0
949	        return len(self.get_finished_seqs())
950	
951	    def is_finished(self) -> bool:
952	        if self.is_single_seq:
953	            return self.first_seq.is_finished()
954	        return all(seq.is_finished() for seq in self.seqs)
955	
956	    def is_prefill(self) -> bool:
957	        return self.first_seq.is_prefill()
958	
959	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
960	        return (f"SequenceGroup(request_id={self.request_id}, "
961	                f"sampling_params={self.sampling_params}, "
962	                f"num_seqs={len(self.seqs)})")
963	
964	    def uses_prompt_embeds(self) -> bool:
965	        """Returns True if the sequence group uses input embeds."""
966	        return any(seq.data.prompt_embeds is not None for seq in self.seqs)
967

990	class SequenceGroupMetadata(
991	        msgspec.Struct,
992	        tag=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
993	        array_like=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
994	        omit_defaults=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
995	    """Metadata for a sequence group. Used to create `AttentionMetadata`.
996	
997	    Args:
998	        request_id: The ID of the request.
999	        is_prompt: Whether the request is at prompt stage.
1000	        seq_data: The sequence data. (Seq id -> sequence data)
1001	        sampling_params: The sampling parameters used to generate the outputs.
1002	        block_tables: The block tables. (Seq id -> list of physical block
1003	            numbers)
1004	        do_sample: True if sampling is required. Sampling is not required when
1005	            e.g., prefill is chunked, and the current iteration only computes
1006	            query tokens for prefill, we don't need sampling.
1007	        token_chunk_size: The number of tokens to be processed (per sequence).
1008	            None if chunking is not required.
1009	        lora_request: LoRA request.
1010	        computed_block_nums: The block numbers that are already computed,
1011	            used in prefix caching.
1012	        state: Internal state tied to this sequence group.
1013	        multi_modal_data: Multi modal data.
1014	        mm_processor_kwargs: Multimodal input processor / mapper overrides.
1015	        encoder_seq_data: Optional sequence data for encoder prompt
1016	                          (SequenceGroup.encoder_seq). Should be None
1017	                          unless you are working with an encoder/decoder
1018	                          model.
1019	        cross_block_table: Optional cross-attention block table associated
1020	                           with the encoder prompt
1021	                           (SequenceGroup.encoder_seq). Should be None
1022	                           unless you are working with an encoder/decoder
1023	                           model.
1024	        prompt_adapter_request: Prompt Adapter request.
1025	    """
1026	
1027	    request_id: str
1028	    is_prompt: bool
1029	    seq_data: dict[int, SequenceData]
1030	    sampling_params: Optional[SamplingParams]
1031	    block_tables: dict[int, list[int]]
1032	    do_sample: bool = True
1033	    pooling_params: Optional[PoolingParams] = None
1034	    lora_request: Optional[LoRARequest] = None
1035	    computed_block_nums: Optional[list[int]] = None
1036	    state: Optional[SequenceGroupState] = msgspec.field(
1037	        default_factory=lambda: SequenceGroupState())
1038	    token_type_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
1039	    multi_modal_data: Optional[MultiModalKwargs] = None

19	@dataclass
20	class SequenceGroupToSample:
21	    # |---------- N-1 iteration --------|
22	    # |---------------- N iteration ---------------------|
23	    # |- tokenA -|......................|-- newTokens ---|
24	    # |---------- context_len ----------|
25	    # |-------------------- seq_len ----------------------|
26	    #                                   |-- query_len ---|
27	
28	    # Sequence ids for the sequence group in a previous step.
29	    seq_ids: list[int]
30	    sampling_params: SamplingParams
31	    # seq_id -> sequence data.
32	    seq_data: dict[int, SequenceData]
33	    # The length of the sequence (all tokens seen in the past + new token to
34	    # compute attention) of the sequence group. None if it is in a decode
35	    # stage.
36	    seq_len: Optional[int]
37	    # The length of new query tokens to compute in the current step. None if it
38	    # is in a decode stage. The length of query_len <= seq_len if chunked
39	    # prefill is enabled.
40	    query_len: Optional[int]
41	    # A random number generator for sampling.
42	    generator: Optional[torch.Generator]
43	    # True if the sequence group is in prefill stage. False if it is in a
44	    # decode stage.
45	    is_prompt: bool
46	    # Query token indices from logits. to compute prompt logprob. Empty if
47	    # prompt logprob is not required.
48	    prompt_logprob_indices: list[int]
49	    # Sample token indices from logits. Empty if sampling is not required.
50	    sample_indices: list[int]
51	
52	    @property
53	    def do_sample(self):
54	        return len(self.sample_indices) > 0
55	
56	    def __post_init__(self):
57	        if len(self.prompt_logprob_indices) > 0:
58	            assert self.sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None
59	        if self.is_prompt:
60	            assert self.seq_len is not None
61	            assert self.query_len is not None
62	
63	
64	def gen_seq_group_to_sample_builder(num_seqs: int):
65	    return lambda: SequenceGroupToSample(
66	        seq_ids=[0] * num_seqs,
67	        sampling_params=None,
68	        seq_data=None,  # type: ignore
69	        seq_len=0,
70	        query_len=0,
71	        generator=None,
72	        is_prompt=True,
73	        prompt_logprob_indices=[],
74	        sample_indices=[],
75	    )

78	class SamplingMetadataCache:
79	    """Used to cache SamplingMetadata objects between scheduler iterations"""
80	
81	    def __init__(self):
82	        self._seq_group_to_sample_cache: dict[int, PyObjectCache] = {}
83	
84	    def get_cached_seq_group_to_sample(self, num_seqs):
85	        if num_seqs not in self._seq_group_to_sample_cache:
86	            self._seq_group_to_sample_cache[num_seqs] = PyObjectCache(
87	                gen_seq_group_to_sample_builder(num_seqs))
88	
89	        obj = self._seq_group_to_sample_cache[num_seqs].get_object()
90	        return obj
91	
92	    def reset(self):
93	        for cache in self._seq_group_to_sample_cache.values():
94	            cache.reset()

97	class SamplingMetadata:
98	    """Metadata for input sequences. Used in sampler.
99	
100	    The usage is as follow;
101	    ```
102	    hidden_states = execute_model(...)
103	    logits = hidden_states[sampling_metadata.selected_token_indices]
104	    sample(logits)
105	
106	    def sample(logits):
107	        # Use categorized_sample_indices for sampling....
108	    ```
109	
110	    Args:
111	        seq_groups: List of batched sequence groups.
112	        selected_token_indices: (num_query_tokens_to_logprob). Indices to find
113	            logits from the initial model output hidden states.
114	        categorized_sample_indices: SamplingType -> token indices to sample.
115	            Each token indices is 2D tensor of (num_indices, num_indices) where
116	            the first item means the sample index within the returned logit
117	            (before pruning padding), and the second item means the sample
118	            index after pruning using selected_token_indices.
119	            For example, if the returned logit is [1, 2, 3], and we select
120	            [1, 2] for sampling, the pruned logit will be [2, 3]. In this case,
121	            The first tuple is [1, 2] (sampled index within original logit),
122	            and the second tuple is [0, 1] (sampled index within pruned logit).
123	        num_prompts: Number of prompt sequence groups in seq_groups.
124	        skip_sampler_cpu_output: Indicates if we want to skip the GPU=>CPU
125	            serialization of token outputs.
126	        reuse_sampling_tensors: Indicates if we want to reuse sampling
127	            tensors that are part of the sampler forward pass. Currently,
128	            it is mainly used for multi-step decode.
129	
130	    """
131	
132	    def __init__(
133	        self,
134	        seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
135	        selected_token_indices: torch.Tensor,
136	        categorized_sample_indices: dict[SamplingType, torch.Tensor],
137	        num_prompts: int,
138	        skip_sampler_cpu_output: bool = False,
139	        reuse_sampling_tensors: bool = False,
140	    ) -> None:
141	        self.seq_groups = seq_groups
142	        self.selected_token_indices = selected_token_indices
143	        self.categorized_sample_indices = categorized_sample_indices
144	        self.num_prompts = num_prompts
145	        self.skip_sampler_cpu_output = skip_sampler_cpu_output
146	        self.reuse_sampling_tensors = reuse_sampling_tensors
147	
148	    @staticmethod
149	    def prepare(
150	        seq_group_metadata_list: list[SequenceGroupMetadata],
151	        seq_lens: list[int],
152	        query_lens: list[int],
153	        device: str,
154	        pin_memory: bool,
155	        generators: Optional[dict[str, torch.Generator]] = None,
156	        cache: Optional[SamplingMetadataCache] = None,
157	    ) -> "SamplingMetadata":
158	        (
159	            seq_groups,
160	            selected_token_indices,
161	            categorized_sample_indices,
162	            num_prompts,
163	        ) = _prepare_seq_groups(seq_group_metadata_list, seq_lens, query_lens,
164	                                device, generators, cache)
165	        selected_token_indices = async_tensor_h2d(
166	            selected_token_indices,
167	            dtype=torch.long,
168	            target_device=device,
169	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
170	        )
171	        categorized_sample_indices = {
172	            t:
173	            async_tensor_h2d(
174	                seq_ids,
175	                dtype=torch.int,
176	                target_device=device,
177	                pin_memory=pin_memory,
178	            )
179	            for t, seq_ids in categorized_sample_indices.items()
180	        }
181	
182	        sampling_metadata = SamplingMetadata(
183	            seq_groups=seq_groups,
184	            selected_token_indices=selected_token_indices,
185	            categorized_sample_indices=categorized_sample_indices,
186	            num_prompts=num_prompts,
187	        )
188	        return sampling_metadata
189	
190	    def __repr__(self) -> str:
191	        return (
192	            "SamplingMetadata("
193	            f"seq_groups={self.seq_groups}, "
194	            f"selected_token_indices={self.selected_token_indices}, "
195	            f"categorized_sample_indices={self.categorized_sample_indices})")
196	
197	
198	def _prepare_seq_groups(
199	    seq_group_metadata_list: list[SequenceGroupMetadata],
200	    seq_lens: list[int],
201	    query_lens: list[int],
202	    device: str,
203	    generators: Optional[dict[str, torch.Generator]] = None,
204	    cache: Optional[SamplingMetadataCache] = None,
205	) -> tuple[
206	        list[SequenceGroupToSample],
207	        list[int],
208	        dict[SamplingType, list[int]],
209	        int,
210	]:
211	    """Prepare sequence groups and indices for sampling.
212	
213	    Args:
214	        seq_group_metadata_list: A list of sequence group to batch.
215	        seq_lens: A list of sequence lens per sequence group.
216	            Index of prompt len should match with seq_group_metadata_list.
217	        query_lens: A list of query lengths. Prompt lens include the length
218	            of entire prompt tokens, and it could be shorter.
219	        device: A device to use for random number generators,
220	            `SequenceGroupToSample.generator`.
221	        generators: A store of per-request random number generators used
222	            for seeded requests.
223	
224	    Returns:
225	        seq_groups: A list of sequence group to sample.
226	        selected_token_indices: See the definition from `SamplingMetadata`.
227	        categorized_sample_indices: See the definition from `SamplingMetadata`.
228	        num_prompts: Total number of prompts from `seq_group_metadata_list`.
229	    """
230	    # Batched sequence groups for the current model forward stsep.
231	    seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample] = []
232	    # A list of token indices to sample/compute logprob. It is used to
233	    # prune the outcome logits from the model for the performance.
234	    selected_token_indices: list[int] = []
235	    # Used for selected_token_indices.
236	    model_output_idx = 0
237	
238	    # Sampling type -> (
239	    # indices to sample/prompt logprob within pruned output logits,
240	    # indices to sample within pruned logits)
241	    categorized_sample_indices: dict[SamplingType, list[int]] = {
242	        t: []
243	        for t in SamplingType
244	    }
245	    # Index of logits to compute logprob. Logits include both prompt logprob
246	    # and sample logprob indices.
247	    logit_idx = 0
248	    # Total number of prompts from given sequence groups.
249	    num_prompts = 0
250	
251	    for i, seq_group_metadata in enumerate(seq_group_metadata_list):
252	        seq_ids = seq_group_metadata.seq_data.keys()
253	
254	        if cache is not None:
255	            sample_obj = cache.get_cached_seq_group_to_sample(len(seq_ids))
256	
257	            for j, seq_id in enumerate(seq_ids):
258	                sample_obj.seq_ids[j] = seq_id
259	
260	            sample_obj.prompt_logprob_indices.clear()
261	            sample_obj.sample_indices.clear()
262	
263	        sampling_params = seq_group_metadata.sampling_params
264	        is_prompt = seq_group_metadata.is_prompt
265	        generator: Optional[torch.Generator] = None
266	        # If the current seq group is in decode stage, it is None.
267	        seq_len: Optional[int] = None
268	        query_len: Optional[int] = None
269	        prompt_logprob_indices: list[int] = (sample_obj.prompt_logprob_indices
270	                                             if cache is not None else [])
271	        sample_indices: list[int] = (sample_obj.sample_indices
272	                                     if cache is not None else [])
273	        do_sample = seq_group_metadata.do_sample
274	
275	        if seq_group_metadata.is_prompt:
276	            if sampling_params.seed is not None:
277	                generator = torch.Generator(device=device).manual_seed(
278	                    sampling_params.seed)
279	                if generators is not None:
280	                    generators[seq_group_metadata.request_id] = generator
281	
282	            num_prompts += 1
283	            num_prefill_sample = len(seq_ids)
284	            assert num_prefill_sample == 1
285	            assert query_lens is not None and seq_lens is not None
286	            query_len, seq_len = query_lens[i], seq_lens[i]
287	            # If we need sampling, exclude num_prefill_sample tokens from
288	            # prompt logprob.
289	            prompt_logprob_len = (query_len - num_prefill_sample
290	                                  if do_sample else query_len)
291	            sample_len = num_prefill_sample if do_sample else 0
292	        else:
293	            # Decode
294	            prompt_logprob_len = 0
295	            query_len = query_lens[i] if query_lens is not None and len(
296	                query_lens) > 0 else 1
297	            sample_len = len(seq_ids) * query_len if do_sample else 0
298	
299	            if sampling_params.seed is not None and generators is not None:
300	                generator = generators.get(seq_group_metadata.request_id)
301	
302	        # Update indices to select from the model output.
303	        """
304	        This blocks computes selected_token_indices which is used in the
305	        following way.
306	
307	        hidden_states = model(...)
308	        logits = hidden_states[selected_token_indices]
309	        """
310	
311	        if sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
312	            selected_token_indices.extend(
313	                range(model_output_idx, model_output_idx + prompt_logprob_len))
314	        model_output_idx += prompt_logprob_len
315	        if do_sample:
316	            selected_token_indices.extend(
317	                range(model_output_idx, model_output_idx + sample_len))
318	        model_output_idx += sample_len
319	
320	        # We now find indices for logprob computation and sampling.
321	        """
322	        This block computes categorized_sample_indices which is used in the
323	        following way.
324	
325	        hidden_states = model(...)
326	        logits = hidden_states[selected_token_indices]
327	        def sample(logits):
328	           # Use categorized_sample_indices for sampling.
329	           # prompt_logprob_indices to find prompt logprob indices.
330	           # sample_indices to find sample indices.
331	        """
332	
333	        if sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
334	            prompt_logprob_indices.extend(
335	                range(logit_idx, logit_idx + prompt_logprob_len))
336	            logit_idx += prompt_logprob_len
337	        if do_sample:
338	            sample_indices.extend(range(logit_idx, logit_idx + sample_len))
339	            categorized_sample_indices[sampling_params.sampling_type].extend(
340	                list(range(logit_idx, logit_idx + sample_len)))
341	            logit_idx += sample_len
342	
343	        if cache is not None:
344	            sample_obj.sampling_params = sampling_params
345	            sample_obj.seq_data = seq_group_metadata.seq_data
346	            sample_obj.seq_len = seq_len
347	            sample_obj.query_len = query_len
348	            sample_obj.generator = generator
349	            sample_obj.is_prompt = is_prompt
350	        else:
351	            sample_obj = SequenceGroupToSample(
352	                seq_ids=list(seq_ids),
353	                sampling_params=sampling_params,
354	                seq_data=seq_group_metadata.seq_data,
355	                seq_len=seq_len,
356	                query_len=query_len,
357	                generator=generator,
358	                is_prompt=is_prompt,
359	                prompt_logprob_indices=list(prompt_logprob_indices),
360	                sample_indices=list(sample_indices),
361	            )
362	
363	        seq_groups.append(sample_obj)
364	
365	    if cache is not None:
366	        cache.reset()
367	
368	    return (seq_groups, selected_token_indices, categorized_sample_indices,
369	            num_prompts)

372	@dataclass
373	class SamplingTensors:
374	    """Tensors for sampling."""
375	
376	    temperatures: torch.Tensor
377	    top_ps: torch.Tensor
378	    top_ks: torch.Tensor
379	    min_ps: torch.Tensor
380	    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
381	    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
382	    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
383	    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
384	    output_tokens: torch.Tensor
385	
386	    @classmethod
387	    def from_sampling_metadata(
388	        cls,
389	        sampling_metadata: "SamplingMetadata",
390	        vocab_size: int,
391	        device: torch.device,
392	        dtype: torch.dtype,
393	    ) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
394	        prompt_tokens: list[array] = []
395	        output_tokens: list[array] = []
396	        top_ks: list[int] = []
397	        temperatures: list[float] = []
398	        top_ps: list[float] = []
399	        min_ps: list[float] = []
400	        presence_penalties: list[float] = []
401	        frequency_penalties: list[float] = []
402	        repetition_penalties: list[float] = []
403	        do_penalties = False
404	        do_top_p_top_k = False
405	        do_min_p = False
406	
407	        assert sampling_metadata.seq_groups is not None
408	        for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
409	            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
410	            sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
411	            temperature = sampling_params.temperature
412	            p = sampling_params.presence_penalty
413	            f = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
414	            r = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
415	            top_p = sampling_params.top_p
416	            min_p = sampling_params.min_p
417	
418	            # k should not be greater than the vocab size.
419	            top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
420	            top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
421	            if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
422	                # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
423	                # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
424	                # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
425	                temperature = 1.0
426	            if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
427	                                       or top_k != vocab_size):
428	                do_top_p_top_k = True
429	            if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
430	                do_min_p = True
431	            if not do_penalties and (abs(p) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
432	                                     or abs(f) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
433	                                     or abs(r - 1.0) >= _SAMPLING_EPS):
434	                do_penalties = True
435	
436	            is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
437	            if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
438	                # For tokens in the prompt that we only need to get
439	                # their logprobs
440	                query_len = seq_group.query_len
441	                assert query_len is not None
442	                prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
443	                temperatures += [temperature] * prefill_len
444	                top_ps += [top_p] * prefill_len
445	                top_ks += [top_k] * prefill_len
446	                min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len
447	                presence_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
448	                frequency_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
449	                repetition_penalties += [1] * prefill_len
450	
451	            if seq_group.do_sample:
452	                sample_lens = len(seq_group.sample_indices)
453	                assert sample_lens >= len(seq_ids)
454	                temperatures += [temperature] * sample_lens
455	                top_ps += [top_p] * sample_lens
456	                top_ks += [top_k] * sample_lens
457	                min_ps += [min_p] * sample_lens
458	                presence_penalties += [p] * sample_lens
459	                frequency_penalties += [f] * sample_lens
460	                repetition_penalties += [r] * sample_lens
461	
462	        if do_penalties:
463	            for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
464	                seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
465	                sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
466	                if (seq_group.is_prompt
467	                        and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None):
468	                    prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
469	                    prompt_tokens.extend(
470	                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
471	                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
472	                    output_tokens.extend(
473	                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
474	                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
475	                if seq_group.do_sample:
476	                    for seq_id in seq_ids:
477	                        seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
478	                        prompt_tokens.append(seq_data.prompt_token_ids_array)
479	                        output_tokens.append(seq_data.output_token_ids_array)
480	
481	        sampling_tensors = SamplingTensors.from_lists(
482	            temperatures,
483	            top_ps,
484	            top_ks,
485	            min_ps,
486	            presence_penalties,
487	            frequency_penalties,
488	            repetition_penalties,
489	            prompt_tokens,
490	            output_tokens,
491	            vocab_size,
492	            device,
493	            dtype,
494	        )
495	        return (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p)
496	
497	    @classmethod
498	    def from_lists(
499	        cls,
500	        temperatures: list[float],
501	        top_ps: list[float],
502	        top_ks: list[int],
503	        min_ps: list[float],
504	        presence_penalties: list[float],
505	        frequency_penalties: list[float],
506	        repetition_penalties: list[float],
507	        prompt_tokens: list[array],
508	        output_tokens: list[array],
509	        vocab_size: int,
510	        device: torch.device,
511	        dtype: torch.dtype,
512	    ) -> "SamplingTensors":
513	        # Note that the performance will be very bad without
514	        # pinned memory.
515	        pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()
516	
517	        do_penalties = prompt_tokens or output_tokens
518	
519	        if do_penalties:
520	            prompt_t = make_tensor_with_pad(
521	                prompt_tokens,
522	                vocab_size,
523	                device="cpu",
524	                dtype=torch.int64,
525	                pin_memory=pin_memory,
526	            )
527	            output_t = make_tensor_with_pad(
528	                output_tokens,
529	                vocab_size,
530	                device="cpu",
531	                dtype=torch.int64,
532	                pin_memory=pin_memory,
533	            )
534	        else:
535	            empty_tensor = torch.empty(0, device=device, dtype=torch.long)
536	            prompt_t = empty_tensor
537	            output_t = empty_tensor
538	
539	        temperatures_t = torch.tensor(
540	            temperatures,
541	            device="cpu",
542	            dtype=dtype,
543	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
544	        )
545	        top_ps_t = torch.tensor(
546	            top_ps,
547	            device="cpu",
548	            dtype=dtype,
549	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
550	        )
551	        min_ps_t = torch.tensor(
552	            min_ps,
553	            device="cpu",
554	            dtype=dtype,
555	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
556	        )
557	        presence_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
558	            presence_penalties,
559	            device="cpu",
560	            dtype=dtype,
561	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
562	        )
563	        frequency_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
564	            frequency_penalties,
565	            device="cpu",
566	            dtype=dtype,
567	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
568	        )
569	        repetition_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
570	            repetition_penalties,
571	            device="cpu",
572	            dtype=dtype,
573	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
574	        )
575	        top_ks_t = torch.tensor(
576	            top_ks,
577	            device="cpu",
578	            dtype=torch.int,
579	            pin_memory=pin_memory,
580	        )
581	        # Because the memory is pinned, we can do non-blocking
582	        # transfer to device.
583	
584	        return cls(
585	            temperatures=temperatures_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
586	            top_ps=top_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
587	            top_ks=top_ks_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
588	            min_ps=min_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
589	            presence_penalties=presence_penalties_t.to(device=device,
590	                                                       non_blocking=True),
591	            frequency_penalties=frequency_penalties_t.to(device=device,
592	                                                         non_blocking=True),
593	            repetition_penalties=repetition_penalties_t.to(device=device,
594	                                                           non_blocking=True),
595	            prompt_tokens=prompt_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
596	            output_tokens=output_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
597	        )

68	@dataclass

86	class SamplerOutput(
87	        msgspec.Struct,
88	        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
89	        array_like=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
90	    """For each sequence group, we generate a list of SequenceOutput object,
91	    each of which contains one possible candidate for the next token.
92	
93	    This data structure implements methods, so it can be used like a list, but
94	    also has optional fields for device tensors.
95	    """
96	
97	    outputs: list[CompletionSequenceGroupOutput]
98	
99	    # On-device tensor containing probabilities of each token.
100	    sampled_token_probs: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
101	
102	    # On-device tensor containing the logprobs of each token.
103	    logprobs: Optional["torch.Tensor"] = None
104	
105	    # Holds either (1) the pythonized sampler result (single-step scheduling)
106	    # or (2) what will be arguments for later deferred pythonization of the
107	    # sampler result (muliti-step scheduling)
108	    deferred_sample_results_args: Optional[SampleResultArgsType] = None
109	
110	    # On-device tensor containing the sampled token ids.
111	    sampled_token_ids: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
112	    # CPU tensor containing the sampled token ids. Used during multi-step to
113	    # return the sampled token ids from last rank to AsyncLLMEngine to be
114	    # 'broadcasted' to all other PP ranks for next step.
115	    sampled_token_ids_cpu: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None

169	class Sampler(nn.Module):
170	    """Samples the next tokens from the model's outputs.
171	
172	    This layer does the following:
173	    1. Discard the hidden states that are not used for sampling (i.e., all
174	        tokens except the final one in each prompt).
175	    2. Compute the logits for the next tokens.
176	    3. Apply presence, frequency and repetition penalties.
177	    4. Apply temperature scaling.
178	    5. Apply top-p and top-k truncation.
179	    6. Sample the next tokens.
180	    Here, each sequence group within the batch can have different sampling
181	    parameters (e.g., sampling method, temperature, top-p, top-k, etc.).
182	
183	    The structure of the logits tensor is coupled with the seq_groups in
184	    sampling_metadata. Typically, each sequence in each seq_group has one row in
185	    logits for the next token to be sampled; however, for a seq_group with a
186	    prompt request with the prompt_logprobs sampling parameter, there are rows
187	    in logits for each token in the input prompt.
188	    """
189	
190	    def __init__(self):
191	        super().__init__()
192	
193	        # Whether or not the SamplerOutput should have on-device tensors
194	        # containing the sampled token ids and probabilities. This is used by
195	        # speculative decoding and when prompt embeddings are specified.
196	        self.include_gpu_probs_tensor = False
197	        self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace = False
198	
199	    def _init_sampling_tensors(
200	        self,
201	        logits: torch.Tensor,
202	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
203	    ):
204	        """The goal here is to reuse sampling tensors between similar decode
205	        runs. This is possible because sampling logic does not change between
206	        decodes of the same sequences.
207	        """
208	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
209	
210	        # First free any existing stored sampling tensors.
211	        # This is necessary because some sampling tensors may
212	        # have pinned memory.
213	        self._sampling_tensors = None
214	
215	        # Initialize new sampling tensors
216	        (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k,
217	         do_min_p) = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(
218	             sampling_metadata, vocab_size, logits.device, logits.dtype)
219	
220	        self._sampling_tensors = sampling_tensors
221	        self._do_penalties = do_penalties
222	        self._do_top_p_top_k = do_top_p_top_k
223	        self._do_min_p = do_min_p
224	
225	    def forward(
226	        self,
227	        logits: torch.Tensor,
228	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
229	    ) -> Optional[SamplerOutput]:
230	        """
231	        Single-step scheduling:
232	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
233	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
234	            * Pythonize sampling result & logprobs tensor
235	
236	        Multi-step scheduling:
237	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
238	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
239	            * Defer Pythonization of sampling result & logprobs
240	            tensor
241	            * Encapsulate arguments required for deferred Pythonization
242	            in the
243	            [`SamplerOutput`][vllm.model_executor.layers.sampler.SamplerOutput]
244	            structure
245	
246	        Args:
247	            logits: (num_tokens, vocab_size).
248	            sampling_metadata: Metadata for sampling.
249	        """
250	        assert logits is not None
251	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
252	
253	        # Prepare sampling tensors with pinned memory to avoid blocking.
254	        if not sampling_metadata.reuse_sampling_tensors:
255	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
256	        elif self._do_penalties:
257	            # In this case, the sampling tensors logic depends on
258	            # "output_tokens" of a sequence. As a result, we cannot
259	            # reuse sampling tensors, since "output_tokens" changes
260	            # between decode runs.
261	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
262	
263	        assert self._sampling_tensors is not None
264	        sampling_tensors = self._sampling_tensors
265	        do_penalties = self._do_penalties
266	        do_top_p_top_k = self._do_top_p_top_k
267	        do_min_p = self._do_min_p
268	
269	        logits = _apply_min_tokens_penalty(logits, sampling_metadata)
270	
271	        # Apply presence and frequency penalties.
272	        if do_penalties:
273	            logits = apply_penalties(logits, sampling_tensors.prompt_tokens,
274	                                     sampling_tensors.output_tokens,
275	                                     sampling_tensors.presence_penalties,
276	                                     sampling_tensors.frequency_penalties,
277	                                     sampling_tensors.repetition_penalties)
278	
279	        # Use float32 to apply temperature scaling.
280	        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
281	        logits = logits.to(torch.float)
282	        logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))
283	
284	        if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
285	            logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
286	                                        sampling_tensors.top_ks)
287	
288	        if do_min_p:
289	            logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)
290	
291	        # We use float32 for probabilities and log probabilities.
292	        # Compute the probabilities.
293	        probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
294	        # Compute the log probabilities.
295	        logprobs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
296	
297	        # Sample the next tokens.
298	        maybe_deferred_sample_results, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor = _sample(
299	            probs,
300	            logprobs,
301	            sampling_metadata,
302	            sampling_tensors,
303	            include_gpu_probs_tensor=self.include_gpu_probs_tensor,
304	            modify_greedy_probs=self._should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace,
305	        )
306	
307	        if self.include_gpu_probs_tensor:
308	            # Since we will defer sampler result Pythonization,
309	            # preserve GPU-side tensors in support of later
310	            # deferred pythonization of logprobs
311	            assert maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor is not None
312	            on_device_tensors = (probs, logprobs, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor)
313	        else:
314	            # Since Pythonization has already happened, don't preserve
315	            # GPU-side tensors.
316	            on_device_tensors = None
317	
318	        # Get the logprobs query results.
319	        prompt_logprobs = None
320	        sample_logprobs = None
321	        if not sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output:
322	            # Pythonize logprobs now (GPU -> CPU); do not defer.
323	            assert not isinstance(maybe_deferred_sample_results,
324	                                  SampleResultArgsType)
325	            prompt_logprobs, sample_logprobs = get_logprobs(
326	                logprobs, sampling_metadata, maybe_deferred_sample_results)
327	
328	        return _build_sampler_output(
329	            maybe_deferred_sample_results,
330	            sampling_metadata,
331	            prompt_logprobs,
332	            sample_logprobs,
333	            on_device_tensors=on_device_tensors,
334	            skip_sampler_cpu_output=sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output)
335	
336	    @property
337	    def _should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace(self) -> bool:
338	        """Whether or not the sampler should modify the probability distribution
339	        of greedily-sampled tokens such that multinomial sampling would sample
340	        the greedily-sampled token.
341	
342	        In other words, if True then we set the probability of the greedily-
343	        sampled token to 1.
344	
345	        This is used by speculative decoding, which requires that the sampling
346	        method be encoded into the probability distribution.
347	        """
348	        return self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace

350	
351	def _apply_min_tokens_penalty(
352	    logits: torch.Tensor,
353	    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
354	) -> torch.Tensor:
355	    """Apply min_tokens penalty which sets stop tokens to -inf if min_tokens
356	        have not been generated yet
357	    """
358	    # list of indices in logits that will be set to -inf
359	    logits_to_penalize: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
360	    logits_applied = 0
361	    for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
362	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
363	        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
364	
365	        sample_indices = seq_group.sample_indices
366	        logits_applied += len(sample_indices) + len(
367	            seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
368	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
369	            continue
370	
371	        start_idx = sample_indices[0]
372	        min_tokens = sampling_params.min_tokens
373	        token_ids_to_penalize = sampling_params.all_stop_token_ids
374	        if min_tokens > 0 and token_ids_to_penalize:
375	            seqs_to_penalize: list[int] = []
376	            for j, seq_id in enumerate(seq_ids):
377	                seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
378	                if len(seq_data.output_token_ids_array) < min_tokens:
379	                    seqs_to_penalize.append(j)
380	
381	            if seqs_to_penalize:
382	                # convert to the index into logits
383	                seqs_to_penalize = [start_idx + j for j in seqs_to_penalize]
384	                # itertools.product pairs each seq index with every token id
385	                logits_to_penalize.extend(
386	                    itertools.product(seqs_to_penalize, token_ids_to_penalize))
387	
388	    if logits_to_penalize:
389	        # use zip and * to group indices along each dimension
390	        # eg. [ (1,2), (1,3), (5,6) ] -> ( (1,1,5), (2,3,6) )
391	        logits[tuple(zip(*logits_to_penalize))] = -float("inf")
392	
393	    # verifies that no rows in logits were missed unexpectedly
394	    assert logits_applied == logits.shape[0]
395	    return logits
396	
397	
398	def _apply_top_k_top_p(
399	    logits: torch.Tensor,
400	    p: torch.Tensor,
401	    k: torch.Tensor,
402	) -> torch.Tensor:
403	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
404	
405	    # Apply top-k.
406	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
407	    # Get all the top_k values.
408	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
409	    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
410	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
411	
412	    # Apply top-p.
413	    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
414	    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
415	    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
416	    # at least one
417	    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
418	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
419	
420	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
421	    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
422	                                                    index=logits_idx,
423	                                                    src=logits_sort)
424	    return logits
425	
426	
427	def _apply_min_p(
428	    logits: torch.Tensor,
429	    min_p: torch.Tensor,
430	) -> torch.Tensor:
431	    """
432	    Adapted from
433	    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
434	    """
435	    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
436	    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
437	    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
438	    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
439	    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
440	
441	    return logits
442
